title: 9 Herbal Essential Oils to Help You Overcome Coughing Fits for Good

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for coughing fits because it has natural soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the throat and reduce irritation.

Eucalyptol is good for coughing fits because it has natural decongestant properties that help break down and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort associated with coughing.
Quercetin is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing symptoms.
Limonene is good for coughing fits because it has natural antitussive properties that help calm and soothe the throat, reducing the urge to cough.

Pinene is good for coughing fits because it has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to open airways and calm the throat, relieving congestion and discomfort associated with coughing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil is often used to help with coughing fits because it has natural properties that can ease and calm the throat and airways. The main reason for this is that the oil contains compounds called eucalyptols, which have a cooling effect when inhaled. When you breathe in eucalyptus oil, these eucalyptols reach the back of your throat and airways, helping to reduce inflammation and congestion. This makes it easier to breathe and can help to calm a cough. Eucalyptus oil also has a decongestant effect, which means it can help to clear out mucus and other debris that may be causing the cough. By reducing congestion and soothing the throat, eucalyptus oil can provide relief from coughing fits. When used in a humidifier or through steam inhalation, the oil can be especially effective at helping to calm a cough and clear out airways.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for coughing fits, you'll need fresh lavender flowers, a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a cheesecloth or a coffee filter. First, harvest and dry fresh lavender flowers in a warm, dry place with good air circulation, making sure they are completely dry and free of moisture. Next, fill the glass jar with the dried lavender flowers, leaving about an inch at the top. Pour coconut or olive oil over the lavender flowers, making sure they are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil extract from the flowers. After the infusion time, strain the oil through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the lavender flowers. The resulting herbal essential oil can be stored in a cool, dark place and used to help soothe coughing fits by inhaling it or mixing a few drops with a carrier oil for a chest rub.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol in LAVANDULA ANGUSTIFOLIA is good for coughing fits because it has natural antitussive properties that help to calm and soothe the throat, reducing inflammation and relieving discomfort associated with coughing.

Carvacrol is good for coughing fits because it has natural antibacterial properties that help to reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing symptoms.
Linalool is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe the irritated airways in the lungs, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing fits.
Rosmarinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the airways, reducing congestion and relieving coughs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as Thyme essential oil, is a natural remedy for coughing fits. It contains a compound called thymol, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When you inhale Thymus vulgaris essential oil, it helps to calm your airways and reduce inflammation in the lungs. This makes it easier to breathe and can help to soothe a cough. Thymus vulgaris essential oil also has a decongestant effect, helping to clear mucus from your airways and loosen phlegm. This can help to break the cycle of coughing and make you feel more comfortable. Additionally, Thymus vulgaris essential oil has a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can make coughing fits worse. This makes it a popular natural remedy for coughs and colds.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils that help with coughing fits, you'll need fresh lavender flowers, a clean glass jar, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a steam distillation apparatus or a simple homemade setup using a pot, a glass container, and a strainer. First, harvest a bunch of fresh lavender flowers and dry them thoroughly in the shade or using a food dehydrator. Then, fill the glass jar with the dried flowers and pour the carrier oil over them, making sure the flowers are completely covered. Let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily, until the oil has turned a lovely purple color and the fragrance has intensified. After that, you'll need to separate the oil from the lavender solids, which can be done using a coffee filter or cheesecloth. If you don't have a steam distillation apparatus, you can make a simple setup at home by placing the glass container with the oil and lavender mixture in a pot of boiling water, then carefully pouring the liquid through a strainer into another container, discarding the solids. This will help you extract the essential oil from the lavender, which can be used to relieve coughing fits due to its calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for coughing fits because it helps to thin and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
Roscmarinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relax.
Menthone is good for coughing fits because it has natural decongestant properties that help relieve congestion and ease coughing.
Limonene is good for coughing fits because it has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.
Linalool is good for coughing fits because it has a calming effect that can help relax the throat and airways, reducing the severity and frequency of coughs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint essential oil, can help relieve coughing fits in several ways. The oil contains a compound called menthol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the throat and calm the muscles that cause coughing. When inhaled, peppermint oil can help relax the airways and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and reducing the need to cough. It also has a cooling effect on the throat, which can help numb the pain and discomfort that can trigger coughing fits. Additionally, peppermint oil's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, further reducing coughing. When applied topically to the chest or throat, peppermint oil can also provide a warm, comforting sensation that can help ease coughing fits.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oil for coughing fits, you'll need dried lavender flowers. First, dry the lavender flowers completely in a warm, dry place or use a food dehydrator on a low temperature setting. Then, place the dried flowers in a clean glass jar and cover them with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. The general ratio is one part dried lavender flowers to three parts carrier oil. Next, seal the jar tightly and leave it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process is called cold infusion, which helps to extract the beneficial compounds from the lavender flowers into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. The resulting essential oil can be used in a diffuser or added to a warm bath to help soothe a cough, but always dilute it with a carrier oil before applying it to the skin.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort of coughing.
Kaempferol is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe and relieve coughs.
Caffeic acid is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relax during coughing episodes.
Tannins, also known as lavender, are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the airways, reducing the severity and frequency of coughs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oil is a natural remedy that can help with coughing fits. This oil is made from the flowers of the Echinacea purpurea plant, also known as coneflower. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation that may be causing the cough. The oil also has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off infections that may be causing the cough. When inhaled, the oil can help calm the body and relax the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea essential oil has expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm that may be causing the cough. This oil can be inhaled directly from a cloth or handkerchief, added to a diffuser, or mixed with a carrier oil for topical application to the chest or throat.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oil that helps with coughing fits, you'll need dried lavender flowers. Start by using a clean glass jar to collect the flowers, and then place them in a clean glass container with a lid. Next, pour a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil over the flowers, making sure they are completely covered. The general rule is to use 1 part flowers to 3 parts carrier oil. Now, place the container in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process is called infusion, where the oil extracts the beneficial properties from the lavender flowers. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Finally, store the Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oil in a dark glass bottle, away from heat and light, and use it as needed to help soothe coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin in LAVANDULA ANGUSTIFOLIA is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of the cough.
Saponins are good for coughing fits because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat and airways, reducing the severity and frequency of coughs.
Licorinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe the throat, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing.
Liquiritin is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing fits.
Flavonoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe the airways, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by coughing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, is a popular herbal remedy for coughs. The essential oil extracted from this plant has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe a sore throat. When we cough, our throat can become irritated and inflamed, making it harder to breathe and swallow. Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil can calm down this inflammation, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with coughing. It also has a natural expectorant property, which helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up. This can provide quick relief from coughing fits, especially those caused by respiratory infections such as bronchitis or pneumonia. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties can help to fight off underlying infections, promoting a faster recovery.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oil that helps with coughing fits, you will need a few simple items. First, dry a bunch of lavender flowers and store them in a cool, dark place until you are ready to use them. Next, fill a clean glass jar with the dried lavender flowers, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Now, pour a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, over the flowers, making sure they are completely covered. Secure the lid on the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar gently every day to help the oil infuse into the flowers. After two weeks, strain the oil into another clean glass bottle, discarding the lavender flowers. This homemade Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oil can be used to help soothe coughs and promote a good night's sleep, but it's essential to remember that it's not a substitute for medical treatment, and you should consult a doctor before using it to treat any health issue.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for coughing fits

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oil, also known as ginger oil, is a natural remedy that can help with coughing fits. This is because ginger oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the throat and reduce swelling. When you have a cough, your throat can become irritated and inflamed, making it harder to breathe and swallow. Ginger oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help calm down the inflammation and reduce the irritation, making it easier to cough up mucus and other debris that's causing the cough. Ginger oil also has expectorant properties, which means it can help loosen and clear out mucus from your lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, ginger oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off infections that can cause coughing fits. By using ginger oil in a diffuser or applying it topically to your chest, you can start to feel relief from your cough and get back to feeling better.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils to help with coughing fits, you'll need a few basic tools and materials. First, you'll need fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers, also known as English lavender, which you can grow yourself or purchase from a local nursery. Next, you'll need a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a steam distiller or a simple homemade setup using a pot, a condenser, and a collection vessel. Start by harvesting the lavender flowers at the peak of their fragrance and drying them thoroughly in a warm, dry place. Once dry, combine the dried flowers with the carrier oil in the glass jar, making sure the flowers are fully submerged. Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days, allowing the oil to infuse and extract the lavender's therapeutic properties. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and leaving you with a fragrant, cough-soothing essential oil that can be used in a diffuser, applied topically (diluted with a carrier oil), or added to a warm bath to help alleviate coughing fits and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils that help with coughing fits, you will need fresh lavender flowers, a carrier oil, and a clean glass jar. First, pick fresh and fragrant lavender flowers from the garden, making sure they are free from any dirt or debris. Then, gently dry the flowers with a clean cloth to remove excess moisture. Next, fill the glass jar about 2/3 full with the dried lavender flowers. Pour a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, over the flowers, making sure they are completely covered. remedies.remedy_desc: Hyptis suaveolens essential oil, also known as Bush Basil, has been used for centuries to help soothe coughing fits. The oil contains a compound called cineole, which is known for its natural expectorant properties. This means it helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. When inhaled, the cineole in Hyptis suaveolens oil can calm the throat and reduce inflammation, helping to ease the discomfort of a cough. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oil that helps with coughing fits, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by harvesting fresh lavender flowers, making sure to choose ones that are free of any signs of disease or pests. Next, dry the flowers thoroughly by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the flowers are completely dry, you can proceed to make the essential oil by filling a clean glass jar about a quarter full with the dried flowers. Then, add a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, covering the flowers completely. Next, place the jar in a saucepan of simmering water, making sure the water level is below the bottom of the jar. Allow the mixture to steep for several hours, or until the oil has taken on the desired lavender color and scent. Finally, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the fragrant essential oil. This lavender oil can be used to help soothe and calm coughs, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oil, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you will need to harvest fresh lavender flowers from a healthy plant, preferably on a dry and sunny day. Next, you will need to dry the flowers thoroughly, either by air-drying them or using a dehydrator, to remove any excess moisture. Once the flowers are completely dry, you can proceed to make the essential oil by using a steam distillation apparatus or a cold press method. To do this, you will need to place the dried flowers in a distillation apparatus or a cold press machine, and then heat the flowers with steam or apply pressure to extract the oil from the plant material. The resulting essential oil can be collected and filtered to remove any impurities. Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oil has been traditionally used to help alleviate coughing fits due to its calming and soothing properties, which can help to calm the throat and reduce inflammation.  (Note: Before making herbal essential oils, make sure to do thorough research and follow proper safety guidelines to ensure the quality and safety of the final product. Also, consult with a healthcare professional or a certified aromatherapist before using any herbal essential oils, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.)

intro_desc: When you have a bad cough, it can be hard to focus on anything else. But did you know that there are some natural remedies that can help? Herbal essential oils, made from plants like Eucalyptus globulus and Thymus vulgaris, are a great way to soothe a sore throat and calm a cough. Eucalyptus globulus, also known as eucalyptus oil, is great because it has a cooling effect that can help ease congestion and make it easier to breathe. This is because eucalyptus oil has a compound called eucalyptol, which is a natural decongestant. Thymus vulgaris, or thyme oil, is also helpful because it has antibacterial properties that can fight off infections that can cause a cough. By using these oils in a diffuser or applying them to your chest, you can start to feel better and reduce the frequency of your coughing fits. Fixing the issue of a persistent cough is important because it can disrupt your daily routine and make it hard to sleep. By using herbal essential oils, you can improve your quality of life and feel more comfortable in your own skin.
supplementary_best: A combination of Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, and Zingiber officinale can help remedy coughing fits. Eucalyptus globulus has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease congestion and help loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up. Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, contains compounds that can kill bacteria and viruses that cause infections, reducing the severity of coughing fits. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has natural expectorant properties that can help thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up. This combination of oils works together to provide multiple benefits: reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and thinning out mucus. When inhaled, the vapors of these oils can help open airways, ease congestion, and calm the body, providing quick relief from coughing fits.
supplementary_avoid: If you have coughing fits, it's best to avoid certain herbal essential oils that can irritate your throat or trigger more coughing. One such oil is 'Sassafras albidum', also known as Sassafras oil, which is derived from the bark of the Sassafras tree. Sassafras oil has been known to cause respiratory problems, including coughing fits, due to its high content of safrole, a compound that can irritate the lungs and airways.  Another oil to be cautious about is 'Asafoetida oil', which is extracted from the Ferula plant. Asafoetida oil has a strong, pungent smell and can be quite irritating to the respiratory system, making it unsuitable for people with coughing fits. Additionally, 'Cassia oil', derived from the Cassia tree, contains a high concentration of coumarin, a compound that can exacerbate respiratory issues and trigger more coughing. Lastly, 'Juniperus virginiana oil', also known as Eastern Red Cedar oil, should be avoided as it can cause irritation and inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it worse for those experiencing coughing fits.
supplementary_other_remedies: Honey is a natural remedy that can help soothe a cough. When you cough, the inside of your throat gets irritated, and this can cause more coughing. Honey has a thick, syrupy texture that coats the inside of your throat, making it feel better and reducing the urge to cough. This is because honey has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm down the irritation in your throat. Additionally, honey is a natural expectorant, which means it helps to bring up mucus from your lungs, making it easier to cough up. Some people also use apple cider vinegar as a natural remedy for coughs. Apple cider vinegar has antibacterial properties that help to kill off any bacteria that may be causing the cough. It also helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up. You can mix apple cider vinegar with water and drink it throughout the day. Another natural remedy is steam inhalation. When you inhale steam, it helps to loosen up mucus in your lungs, making it easier to cough up. You can do this by taking a hot shower or by using a bowl of hot water and leaning over it with a towel over your head. This helps to trap the steam and direct it towards your face. The moisture from the steam also helps to soothe an irritated throat, reducing the urge to cough.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have coughing fits may also experience other ailments due to the underlying causes. For example, if the cough is caused by a bacterial infection, they may also have a fever. This is because the body is fighting the infection, which can raise the body temperature. Medicinal herbs like yarrow and elderflower have antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the infection and fever.  If the cough is caused by allergies or asthma, people may experience congestion and difficulty breathing. Medicinal herbs like eucalyptus and thyme have decongestant properties that can help open up airways and relieve congestion. They can also reduce inflammation and make it easier to breathe.  If the cough is caused by a viral infection, people may experience fatigue and a sore throat. Medicinal herbs like licorice root and slippery elm have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the throat and reduce inflammation. They can also help boost the immune system to fight off the infection.  If the cough is caused by a cold or flu, people may experience body aches and headaches. Medicinal herbs like willow bark and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation. They can also help reduce fever and promote sweating to help the body get rid of the infection.  In these cases, medicinal herbs can provide relief from the underlying causes of the coughing fits, rather than just treating the symptoms. They can help the body heal and recover faster, and reduce the risk of complications.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for coughing fits
